items.q: Project Infinity says "mod list doesn't contain such mod ID"
items.a: This happens when your WeiDU.log references a mod that PI doesn't have in its download database. Common example: DW_TALENTS (Talents of Faerun). Download the mod manually from its GitHub releases page and place the folder in your game directory. PI will then detect it on the next scan.

items.q: Which installer should I use?
items.a:
For automated installs, use mod_installer (github.com/dark0dave/mod_installer) v14.5+ — it can read the WeiDU.log files exported from Infinity Mod Forge and install all mods automatically. Use the "eet" command with --bg1-game-directory and --bg2-game-directory flags. IMPORTANT: versions before v14 have a bug that causes hangs during EET installs. Always use the latest release.

Project Infinity can also import WeiDU.log files and handle mod downloading.

For manual installs, use WeiDU directly via command line. Note: Gideon's Install Tool has been discontinued.

items.q: How do I use mod_installer with Infinity Mod Forge?
items.a:
1) Export WeiDU.log and WeiDU-BGEE.log from Infinity Mod Forge
2) Download mod_installer v14.5+ from github.com/dark0dave/mod_installer/releases
3) Place all mod archives in one directory (e.g., Mods/)
4) Run: mod_installer eet --bg1-game-directory "path/to/BG1EE" --bg2-game-directory "path/to/BG2EE" --bg1-log-file WeiDU-BGEE.log --bg2-log-file WeiDU.log --mod-directories "path/to/Mods" --weidu-binary "path/to/weidu.exe" --never-abort true
5) Missing mods will be skipped. Use --download true to be prompted for download URLs.

Tips:
• Use --never-abort true to continue past individual mod failures
• Some mods need their tp2 moved into the mod subfolder (e.g., Tenya Thermidor)
• The "Mod Download List" export gives you URLs for all selected mods

items.q: WeiDU codepage bug (v247–249)
items.a: WeiDU versions 24700–24900 have a codepage handling bug that can cause character encoding errors during installation. Upgrade to WeiDU 24901+ or downgrade to 24600.

items.q: Mod fails with "PARSE ERROR" or "Failure" during install
items.a: Usually caused by a mod version incompatible with the current game patch, or by installing mods in the wrong order. Check that you're using the latest version of the mod and that EET prerequisites (DLC Merger, EE Fixpack, EET) are installed first in the correct order.

items.q: What's the correct install order for EET essentials?
items.a:
1) DLC Merger (on BG2:EE)
2) EE Fixpack (install on BOTH BG1:EE and BG2:EE)
3) Any Pre-EET BG1 mods (on BG1:EE)
4) EET (on BG2:EE — this merges BG1 into BG2)
5) All other mods (on the merged BG2:EE game)
6) EET_End (last, to finalize)

items.q: EET install fails or hangs
items.a:
Common causes:
• BG1:EE and BG2:EE are not at the same patch version
• DLC Merger was not run first
• A Pre-EET mod is incompatible with EET
• Insufficient disk space (EET needs ~5GB free)
• Game files are in a protected directory (Program Files). Move the game to a non-protected location.

items.q: Do I need to start a new game after modding?
items.a: Yes — always start a fresh game after installing mods. Mid-game mod changes will cause crashes, broken quests, and save corruption. The only exceptions are pure cosmetic mods (portraits, sounds) that don't modify game scripts or dialogues.

items.q: Can I install mods on macOS or Linux?
items.a: Yes. All standard WeiDU mods are cross-platform. On macOS, install WeiDU for Mac and use Terminal. On Linux, you need a case-insensitive filesystem (ciopfs or ext4 with casefold). Only mods marked WIN (requiring EEex) are Windows-only (or Linux via Proton).

items.q: What does "DUAL INSTALL" mean for EE Fixpack?
items.a: EE Fixpack must be installed on both your BG1:EE and BG2:EE game directories before running EET. EET copies the modded BG1 into BG2, so both need the fixes applied.

items.q: Mods marked "Standalone" or with the 📱 filter
items.a: These mods work without EET on standalone BG1:EE, BG2:EE, or mobile. If you're doing an EET install, most mods work fine. The Standalone filter helps iOS/Android players find compatible mods.

items.q: What do the conflict severities mean?
items.a:
HARD: These mods are mutually exclusive — install only one.
PARTIAL: Specific components overlap. Often solvable with install order or by skipping conflicting components.
SOFT: Minor overlap or historical issue. Usually safe to install both with correct ordering.

items.q: A mod says "Functionality provided by X"
items.a: This means another mod in your selection already includes the same feature. You can safely skip the conflicting mod or its overlapping components. The side panel (click Info) shows which specific components conflict.

items.q: How do I read the conflict alerts at the top?
items.a: Red alerts (●) are hard conflicts — you should remove one of the mods. Orange alerts (●) are soft/partial — usually fine with correct install order. Click any mod name in the alert to jump to it. Click Info for full conflict details in the side panel.

items.q: Game crashes on startup after modding
items.a:
Common causes:
• Conflicting UI mods (only install one major UI overhaul)
• Missing EET_End (must be the very last mod installed)
• Corrupted mod files — re-download and reinstall
• Outdated mods incompatible with current game version
• Try running Generalized Biffing as the final step to fix file loading issues

items.q: Game crashes during combat or spellcasting
items.a:
Likely the SPLSTATE.IDS limit. This file has a hardcoded 256-entry limit. SCS adds ~40 entries, EE Fixpack ~20, kit mods add variable amounts. If you exceed ~250 entries, the game crashes on spell-related actions.

Check: Open override/SPLSTATE.IDS and count entries. If near 256, remove large kit mods or Enhanced Powergaming Scripts.

items.q: Game is slow or takes long to load
items.a: Large mod installs create thousands of override files. Install Generalized Biffing after EET_End to compress these into BIFF archives, dramatically improving load times.

items.q: Dialogue is broken or NPCs say wrong things
items.a: Usually caused by wrong install order for NPC/dialogue mods. Quest mods should install before NPC mods, and NPC mods before tweaks. Check the Order overlay for the correct sequence.

items.q: WeiDU error: "DELETE_BYTES out of bounds"
items.a:
A mod tried to modify a file that's a different size than expected because another mod already changed it. This is a mod ordering issue.

Fix: Move the failing mod earlier in the install sequence so it patches the file before other mods touch it. Klatu's item components (#2080, #2090) are especially prone to this.

items.q: Items appearing twice (duplication)
items.a: Usually caused by overlapping import components. Example: EET Tweaks "Import party items to SoA" (#4040) + SoD2BG2_IU "Force all import-able SoD items" (#8) together cause SoD items to appear twice. Choose one import method, not both.

items.q: WeiDU can't find game folder
items.a:
Ensure your game is in a standard path without spaces or special characters. Don't install in Program Files (Windows) or use network drives.

Good: C:\Games\BG2EE
Bad: C:\Program Files (x86)\Baldur's Gate II

items.q: Linux: mods fail with "file not found" errors
items.a:
Linux filesystems are case-sensitive but Infinity Engine uses mixed case. You need a case-insensitive filesystem:
• ext4 with casefold enabled
• ciopfs overlay mount
• Or run the game through Wine/Proton

items.q: Kit Cap exceeded (320 limit)
items.a: The Infinity Engine supports a maximum of 320 kits. If your selection exceeds ~300, remove some kit mods. The Spells & Kits > Overview shows which mods add the most kits. Deities of Faerun (123 kits) and Faiths and Powers (97 kits) are the biggest contributors.

items.q: SPLSTATE.IDS full (256 limit)
items.a: Similar to Kit Cap — the SPLSTATE counter shows which mods consume slots. Reduce spell/kit mods if approaching the limit. The Spells & Kits > Overview tracks this in real time as you toggle mods.

items.q: Spell IDs per level approaching 50
items.a: Each spell level (Wizard 1–9, Priest 1–7) has a ~50-slot limit. The Spells & Kits > Overview shows per-level usage. If a level is near capacity, avoid adding more mods that introduce spells at that level.

items.q: Mod installs but features don't work
items.a: Some mods require specific install order relative to others. Check the notes on each component in Infinity Mod Forge. "Last installed wins" for mods that modify the same files — the Order overlay shows this. Some components are marked "Requires SR" (Spell Revisions) — skip these if you don't have SR installed.

items.q: "[*.IDS] forgotten"
items.a: This is normal. WeiDU is clearing its in-memory cache of IDS files (ACTION.IDS, TRIGGER.IDS, etc.) so they get re-read from disk. Mods do this after modifying IDS files to ensure subsequent operations see the new entries. Completely harmless.

items.q: "Not copying [X] because it did not change"
items.a: Normal and harmless. A mod applied a patch to a file but the result was identical to the original. WeiDU skips writing the file to save disk I/O. Common with COPY_EXISTING_REGEXP operations that match thousands of files but only modify a few.

items.q: "WARNING: [X.BCS] cannot be decompiled and recompiled (round-trip failure)"
items.a: A BCS (compiled script) file uses actions or triggers that aren't in the current IDS files, so WeiDU can't safely decompile and recompile it. WeiDU preserves the original file unchanged. This is a known WeiDU limitation with scripts modified by multiple mods. Usually harmless, but can occasionally cause issues with mods that need to patch that script.

items.q: "LEXER ERROR" or "Parsing.Parse_error" on a .BCS file
items.a: The script file has invalid format — either it was corrupted by a previous mod's failed round-trip, or it uses syntax WeiDU doesn't understand. If this causes a mod to fail, the script may need a pre-install patch. Known issue with RR#ARAN2.BCS (Rogue Rebalancing) and some Golem Construction scripts.

items.q: "Unable to Unlink" errors during rollback
items.a: Windows file locking prevented WeiDU from deleting a backup file during error recovery. This is cosmetic — the rollback still works, just leaves some orphaned backup files in weidu_external/backup/. These are safe to delete manually after the install completes.

items.q: "Copying and patching 14000+ files"
items.a: A mod is running COPY_EXISTING_REGEXP, which processes all matching files in the override directory. Large installs (300+ mods) can have 14,000–20,000 files in override. This operation is slow (1–5 minutes) but normal. Common culprits: SCS, Talents of Faerun (SFO library), and any mod that patches all items/spells/creatures.

items.q: "ERROR: [X.BCS] -> [weidu_external/workspace/discard.baf] Patching Failed"
items.a: A mod tried to patch a BCS script that couldn't be decompiled. If the mod has an ALWAYS block with BCS round-trip guards, this error is caught and the original script is preserved. If not, the component will fail. This is the most common cause of install failures in large mod setups.

items.q: "Stopping installation because of error" (repeated many times)
items.a: WeiDU prints this once for each pending operation that gets cancelled after an error. Seeing 10–20 of these in a row is normal — it's just WeiDU unwinding its operation stack. The actual error is the line BEFORE the first "Stopping" message.

tips.title: Start small
tips.desc: Use "First Adventure" or "Seasoned Adventurer" before the mega-install.

tips.title: Read conflict alerts
tips.desc: A hard conflict means the game will likely break.

tips.title: Back up your clean game
tips.desc: Copy the entire game folder before modding.

tips.title: Save your selection
tips.desc: Use "Share" for a URL or "Save As" for a local preset.

tips.title: Don't panic about soft conflicts
tips.desc: Orange alerts usually mean "install order matters", not "game will break".

tips.title: EET_End must be last
tips.desc: Always. No exceptions. The app enforces this in exports.

tips.title: New game required
tips.desc: EET creates a merged game. Old saves won't work.

prefixRules.title: Length
prefixRules.desc: 2 characters is standard. 3 is acceptable for projects. Longer wastes precious resref space (max 8 chars).

prefixRules.title: Safe characters
prefixRules.desc: Letters, digits, and symbols like # ! _ - are fine. Avoid regex special characters: $ ^ . * + ? [ ] \ — these break WeiDU variable matching.

prefixRules.title: Case
prefixRules.desc: Prefixes are case-insensitive on Windows. A7 and a7 would collide.

prefixRules.title: Areas
prefixRules.desc: A 2-char prefix leaves 4 characters for area naming (6-char limit). A 3-char prefix leaves only 3.

prefixRules.title: Uniqueness
prefixRules.desc: Check both Project and Personal lists below. Some prefixes appear twice due to unresolved conflicts.
